name: looprelay
description: Local continuity and evidence for long-running Codex and Claude Code loops.

interface.shortDescription: Local continuity and evidence for long-running coding-agent loops
interface.longDescription: LoopRelay preserves safe session, worktree, branch, request, and outcome evidence locally so Codex and Claude Code work can resume without rediscovery. It creates continuation briefs, detects repeated failure patterns, and turns only user-approved lessons into memory or instruction proposals.
defaultPrompt: Set up LoopRelay for this machine
Check whether LoopRelay is ready for the latest Codex session
Recover the current worktree and branch state
Create a continuation brief for the next session
Record the outcome of the latest request
Show recurring failure patterns across recent loops
Propose an approved lesson for memory or AGENTS.md
Diagnose what information is missing before this request can proceed
Open my local LoopRelay evidence archive
